req.title: External ACH transfer (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-079 defines three distinct behavioural zones: (1) valid transfers ≤ $10,000 that must be queued and confirmed, (2) transfers > $10,000 without step-up that must be rejected with 422, and (3) idempotency semantics requiring identical responses on duplicate Idempotency-Key submissions. Boundary values at exactly $10,000 and $10,001 are critical. Insufficient-funds (409) and the idempotency replay path complete coverage of the stated error pathways and success invariants. Five tests are generated covering EP, BVA, NEG, DT, and STC heuristics respectively.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
No prior transfer with the same Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00, valid ach_account_id, and unique Idempotency-Key
trace: R-FIN-079: transfers up to $10,000 shall be accepted and ACH batch queued
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queued(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "ach_account_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_account_id"],
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert <confirm_event_exists>(body["transfer_id"])

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_stepup_returns_422
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
No step-up verification token present in request
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and no step-up header
expected: Response body error code indicates step-up required
trace: R-FIN-079: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification
R-FIN-079: 422 error pathway for missing step-up

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exactly_at_limit_accepted
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
No prior transfer with the same Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.00 (exactly at the $10,000 boundary)
trace: R-FIN-079: upper inclusive boundary of $10,000 must be accepted without step-up
BVA on-point at maximum allowed value

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_stepup_rejected
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
No step-up verification token present in request
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 (one cent above boundary) and no step-up header
trace: BVA off-point immediately above maximum allowed value

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original_response
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
A prior successful transfer has been submitted with a known Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=1000.00 and Idempotency-Key=<known_key> (first submission)
POST /transfers/ach with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
expected: Both responses contain the same transfer_id
Both responses contain the same status value
No duplicate ACH batch entry created
trace: R-FIN-079: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
STC: system transitions from 'pending' to 'queued' only once; replay must not trigger a new transition
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original_response(api_client, verified_ach_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "ach_account_id": verified_ach_customer["ach_account_id"],
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_ach_customer['token']}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp1 = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 202
    body1 = resp1.json()
    resp2 = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 202
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body1["transfer_id"] == body2["transfer_id"]
    assert body1["status"] == body2["status"]
    assert <count_ach_batch_entries>(body1["transfer_id"]) == 1

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer account exists with verified ACH link
Customer balance is $0.00 (or below requested transfer amount)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=500.00 where customer balance < 500.00
expected: Response body error code indicates insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-079: 409 on insufficient funds error pathway
DT: condition combination — valid amount AND insufficient balance → 409
